Sub CreateFile(strName As String)
    Dim thisWb As Workbook
    Dim wbName As String
    Dim wbName_new As String
    Dim g_dtNow         As Date
    
    g_dtNow = Now
    
    Set thisWb = ActiveWorkbook
    wbName = ActiveWorkbook.Name
      
    Dim DirFile As String    ' check to see if file already exists
    
    DirFile = thisWb.Path & "\" & strName & ".xlsx"

If Len(Dir(DirFile)) = 0 Then
    Workbooks.Add
    ActiveWorkbook.SaveAs fileName:=thisWb.Path & "\" & strName
    
    wbName_new = ActiveWorkbook.Name

    Workbooks(wbName).Activate
    Sheets("OUTPUT").Select
    Range("A1").Select
    Range(Selection, ActiveCell.SpecialCells(xlLastCell)).Select
    Selection.Copy
    Workbooks(wbName_new).Activate
    Sheets("Sheet1").Select
    Selection.PasteSpecial Paste:=xlValues, Operation:=xlNone, SkipBlanks:= _
        False, Transpose:=False
    Selection.PasteSpecial Paste:=xlPasteColumnWidths, Operation:=xlNone, _
        SkipBlanks:=False, Transpose:=False
    Selection.PasteSpecial Paste:=xlPasteFormats, Operation:=xlNone, _
        SkipBlanks:=False, Transpose:=False
    Range("A1").Select
    Application.CutCopyMode = False
    ActiveCell.FormulaR1C1 = "Recorded Output  " & g_dtNow
    Range("A1").Select
    Selection.Font.Bold = True
    Selection.Font.ColorIndex = 3
    Windows.Arrange ArrangeStyle:=xlVertical
    Workbooks(wbName).Activate
    Range("A1").Select
Else
    MsgBox "File Already Exists, Enter Unique Name"
End If
    
End Sub

Private Sub ShowOneAutosize(UsePressure As Boolean)
    Dim PressureSetting As MsoTriState
    Dim GravitySetting As MsoTriState
    
    If UsePressure Then
        PressureSetting = msoCTrue
        GravitySetting = msoFalse
    Else
        PressureSetting = msoFalse
        GravitySetting = msoCTrue
    End If
    
    With Me
        .Shapes("cmdAutosize").Visible = PressureSetting
        .Shapes("cmdAutosizeA").Visible = GravitySetting
    End With
End Sub

Attribute VB_Name = "WBSCompSelect"
Option Explicit

' CompSelect.bas: Component Selection module, based on the component selection
' algorithm in cu-life.
'
' The CompSelect macro is the entry point to all of this code.  It uses the
' appropriate priority lookup column for the component level and system size
' category to fill in the entries for the Use column.  Possible entries for
' Use include the following (defined in constants, below):
'   "" - (blank) No component or zero quantity on this row
'    1 - use this component
'    0 - don't use this component
'    x - no component was available for this WBS; cu-life should issue a
'        "contact vendor" error
'   ec - Excel error in the cost for this component; cu-life should issue
'        the corresponding error
'   ep - Excel error in the priority lookup for this component; cu-life should'
'        issue the corresponding error
' The only numeric entries are 1 and 0, so that we can use the SUMPRODUCT
' worksheet function to compute the total direct cost of the system.

Private Const USE_YES As Long = 1
Private Const USE_NO As Long = 0
Private Const USE_UNAVAIL As String = "x"
Private Const USE_COSTERR As String = "ec"
Private Const USE_PRIERR As String = "ep"

Private Const COL_WBS As Long = 1
Private Const COL_QTY As Long = 3
Private Const COL_TOT_COST As Long = 10
Private Const COL_USE As Long = 12
Private Const COL_PRI_START As Long = 14

Public Sub CompSelect()
    Dim wsOutput As Worksheet
    Dim rngOutput As Range
    Dim vntOutput As Variant
    Dim lPriLookupCol As Long
    Dim rngUseCol As Range
    Dim lNRows As Long
    Dim lFirstRow As Long, lLastRow As Long
    Dim DidIncrementRow As Boolean
    Dim dQuantity As Double
    Dim sThisWBS As String
    Dim vntUse() As Variant
    Dim rngUse As Range
    Dim lRowIndx As Long
    Dim lCompRow As Long
    
    Set wsOutput = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("OUTPUT")
    With wsOutput
        Set rngOutput = .Range("output_db")
    End With
    With rngOutput
        vntOutput = .Value
        lNRows = .Rows.Count
        Set rngUse = .Columns(COL_USE)
    End With
    
    ' Determine what priority lookup column to use
    lPriLookupCol = GetPriLookupCol()
    If lPriLookupCol = 0 Then Exit Sub  ' couldn't read component level or system size, no point in continuing
    
    ' Prepare the "use" column by setting all cells to Empty, to begin with
    ReDim vntUse(1 To lNRows, 1 To 1) As Variant
    For lRowIndx = 1 To lNRows
        vntUse(lRowIndx, 1) = Empty
    Next lRowIndx
    
    ' Get ready to loop through the output, selecting components
    lFirstRow = 1
    Do
        DidIncrementRow = False
        ' Check whether the current FirstRow points to an item with nonzero qty
        dQuantity = SafeVntValueD(vntOutput(lFirstRow, COL_QTY))
        If dQuantity > 0# Then
            ' Now make sure there is a valid WBS
            sThisWBS = SafeVntValueS(vntOutput(lFirstRow, COL_WBS))
            If Len(sThisWBS) > 0 Then
                ' Find the last row with the same WBS number
                lLastRow = lFirstRow
                Do
                    lLastRow = lLastRow + 1
                Loop Until SafeVntValueS(vntOutput(lLastRow, 1)) <> sThisWBS
                lLastRow = lLastRow - 1
                
                ' now we know what set of components to consider; select the
                ' correct one, and fill vntUse appropriately, with
                ' GetComponentRow.
                Call GetComponentRow(vntOutput, lPriLookupCol, _
                                     lFirstRow, lLastRow, vntUse)
                
                ' Indicate that we incremented LastRow.
                DidIncrementRow = True
            End If ' wbs is available
        End If ' quantity is numeric
        
        ' Reset LastRow, if necessary.
        If Not DidIncrementRow Then
            lLastRow = lFirstRow
        End If
        ' And set FirstRow one past LastRow.
        lFirstRow = lLastRow + 1
    Loop While lFirstRow < lNRows
    
    ' Now that we're through the component selection loop, drop vntUse into the
    ' appropriate column.  The formula for direct cost will do the rest.
    rngUse.Value = vntUse
    
    '***jmfd added StoreInputs call 2-17-06
    ' Finally, store the inputs we used to generate this cost.
    Call StdDesign.StoreInputs
End Sub

' PriLookupCol looks at the system size and component level (as set in the
' ranges ss_cat2 and component_level) to determine which priority lookup column
' we should use for component selection.  It is called once at the beginning of
' CompSelect.  It returns the column number within the range output_db.  If
' there is some problem with the size or component level (not impossible, since
' component_level is a user input) it brings up an error dialog and returns 0.
Private Function GetPriLookupCol() As Long
    Dim rngSize As Range
    Dim rngCompLevel As Range
    Dim lSizeCat As Long        ' system size: 1 small, 2 mid, 3 large
    Dim lCompLevelCat As Long   ' comp level: "" (0) lo, 1 mid, 2 hi
    Dim lPriCol As Long
    Dim HitBadValue As Boolean
    
    HitBadValue = False
    With ThisWorkbook.Names
        Set rngSize = .Item("ss_cat2").RefersToRange
        Set rngCompLevel = .Item("component_level").RefersToRange
    End With
    lSizeCat = SafeVntValueL(rngSize.Cells(1, 1).Value)
    lCompLevelCat = SafeVntValueL(rngCompLevel.Cells(1, 1).Value)
    
    ' Find the Lo column for the system size...
    Select Case lSizeCat
    Case 1
        lPriCol = COL_PRI_START
    Case 2
        lPriCol = COL_PRI_START + 3
    Case 3
        lPriCol = COL_PRI_START + 6
    Case Else
        HitBadValue = True
    End Select
    
    ' ...and modify per the component level.
    Select Case lCompLevelCat
    Case 0
        ' do nothing
    Case 1
        lPriCol = lPriCol + 1
    Case 2
        lPriCol = lPriCol + 2
    Case Else
        HitBadValue = True
    End Select
    
    If HitBadValue Then
        Call MsgBox("Component Selection: Couldn't find system size or component level.", _
                    vbOKOnly + vbCritical, ThisWorkbook.Name)
        GetPriLookupCol = 0
    Else
        GetPriLookupCol = lPriCol
    End If
End Function

' GetComponentRow looks at the set of component rows in output_db, between
' FirstRow and LastRow (inclusive).  It uses the priorities in PriColumn and
' the total costs to determine which component should be chosen.  It returns
' its results through entries in the Use() variant, which are suitable to be
' dropped into rngUse above.
Private Sub GetComponentRow(Output As Variant, PriColumn As Long, _
                            FirstRow As Long, LastRow As Long, _
                            Use() As Variant)
    Dim lNRows As Long
    Dim lPriorities() As Long
    Dim dCosts() As Double
    Dim lThisPri As Long
    Dim lBAPriority As Long
    Dim lRowIndx As Long
    Dim IsCompChosen As Boolean
    Dim lChosenRow As Long
    Dim FoundZero As Boolean
    Dim BlankCell As Boolean
    Dim XlError As Boolean
    
    ' Set up for priority lookup.
    lNRows = LastRow - FirstRow + 1
    ReDim lPriorities(FirstRow To LastRow) As Long
    ReDim dCosts(FirstRow To LastRow) As Double
    
    ' First, load the priorities and costs into typed arrays.  We also
    ' determine here whether there is a zero in the priority set for this WBS,
    ' and assign the Excel error codes in Use() if needed.
    FoundZero = False
    For lRowIndx = FirstRow To LastRow
        lPriorities(lRowIndx) = SafeVntValueL(Output(lRowIndx, PriColumn), _
                                              BlankCell, XlError)
        If XlError Then
            Use(lRowIndx, 1) = USE_PRIERR
        ElseIf Not BlankCell And lPriorities(lRowIndx) = 0 Then
            FoundZero = True
        End If
        dCosts(lRowIndx) = SafeVntValueD(Output(lRowIndx, COL_TOT_COST), _
                                         BlankCell, XlError)
        ' allow a cost error to overwrite a priority error
        If XlError Then Use(lRowIndx, 1) = USE_COSTERR
    Next lRowIndx
    
    lBAPriority = 1
    IsCompChosen = False
    Do
        For lRowIndx = FirstRow To LastRow
            If lPriorities(lRowIndx) = lBAPriority Then
                If dCosts(lRowIndx) > 0 Then
                    lChosenRow = lRowIndx
                    IsCompChosen = True
                    Exit For
                End If ' total cost is available
            End If ' priority is best available
        Next lRowIndx
        lBAPriority = lBAPriority + 1
    Loop Until IsCompChosen Or lBAPriority > lNRows
    
    ' now that we've determined whether to use a component, store the
    ' appropriate values in Use()
    For lRowIndx = FirstRow To LastRow
        ' make sure we aren't overwriting an error code
        If IsEmpty(Use(lRowIndx, 1)) Then
            If IsCompChosen Then
                ' store yes/no for the chosen/discarded components
                If lRowIndx = lChosenRow Then
                    Use(lRowIndx, 1) = USE_YES
                Else
                    Use(lRowIndx, 1) = USE_NO
                End If
            Else
                ' store no's to suppress cu-life error message, or unavail's
                ' to cause a "contact vendor" message
                If FoundZero Then
                    Use(lRowIndx, 1) = USE_NO
                Else
                    Use(lRowIndx, 1) = USE_UNAVAIL
                End If
            End If
        End If
    Next lRowIndx
End Sub

' SafeVntValueL: Get the value of a particular Variant, as a Long.  Returns
' zero if any of the following is true:
' - vnt contains an Excel error (in this case the optional argument XlError is
'   also set to true)
' - vnt is Empty (in this case the optional argument BlankCell is also set to
'   true)
' - vnt does not contain a numeric value
Private Function SafeVntValueL(vnt As Variant, _
                               Optional ByRef BlankCell As Boolean, _
                               Optional ByRef XlError As Boolean) As Long
    XlError = False
    BlankCell = False
    
    If IsError(vnt) Then
        SafeVntValueL = 0
        XlError = True
    ElseIf IsEmpty(vnt) Then
        SafeVntValueL = 0
        BlankCell = True
    ElseIf Not IsNumeric(vnt) Then
        SafeVntValueL = 0
    Else
        SafeVntValueL = vnt
    End If
End Function

' SafeVntValueD: Get the value of a particular Variant, as a Double.  Returns
' zero if any of the following is true:
' - vnt contains an Excel error (in this case the optional argument XlError is
'   also set to true)
' - vnt is Empty (in this case the optional argument BlankCell is also set to
'   true)
' - vnt does not contain a numeric value
Private Function SafeVntValueD(vnt As Variant, _
                               Optional ByRef BlankCell As Boolean, _
                               Optional ByRef XlError As Boolean) As Double
    XlError = False
    
    If IsError(vnt) Then
        SafeVntValueD = 0#
        XlError = True
    ElseIf IsEmpty(vnt) Then
        SafeVntValueD = 0#
        BlankCell = True
    ElseIf Not IsNumeric(vnt) Then
        SafeVntValueD = 0#
    Else
        SafeVntValueD = vnt
    End If
End Function

' SafeVntValueS: Get the value of a particular Variant, as a String.  Returns
' "" if any of the following is true:
' - vnt contains an Excel error (in this case the optional argument XlError is
'   also set to true)
Private Function SafeVntValueS(vnt As Variant, _
                               Optional ByRef XlError As Boolean) As String
    XlError = False
    
    If IsError(vnt) Then
        SafeVntValueS = ""
        XlError = True
    Else
        SafeVntValueS = vnt
    End If
End Function

    rngWidth As Range
    rngBedDepth As Range

Private Type InputValuesType

    dLength As Double
    dBedDepth As Double
End Type

'
' Sub AutoSize_Opt
' Jon Fetter-Degges, 3/2005
'
Public Sub AutoSize_OptA(Optional Interactive As Boolean = False)
    ' Worksheets
    Dim ComputedSheet As Worksheet
    Dim wsMainInput As Worksheet
    Dim wsOutput As Worksheet
    ' Ranges on the Output sheet
    Dim rngDirectCost As Range
    Dim rngValidInput As Range          ' machine-readable input checks
    ' Ranges on the Autosize sheet
    Dim rngMinVert As Range             ' "min number for vertical vessel" cell on the autosize sheet
    Dim rngAddlNumber As Range          ' "extra search" cell on the autosize sheet
    Dim rngNLines As Range              ' "number of lines" range on autosize sheet
    Dim rngTargetLoadIn As Range
    Dim ComputedInputs As ComputedInputRangesType
    Dim rngSuccess As Range
    ' Ranges and values on the Input sheet
    Dim OldInputs As InputValuesType
    Dim MainInputs As MainInputRangesType
    ' Values from the Autosize sheet
    Dim lExtraSearch As Long
    Dim lMinVertNLines As Long
    ' Variables used in the search
    Dim lNLines As Long
    Dim lNSinceMin As Long
    Dim lNInvalid As Long
    Dim dMinCostVert As Double
    Dim lNLines_MinCostVert As Long
    ' Results
    Dim AutosizeSucceeded As Boolean
    Dim vntDirectCost As Variant
    Dim dDirectCost As Double

    With ThisWorkbook
        Set ComputedSheet = .Worksheets("AutoSize")
        Set wsMainInput = .Worksheets("INPUT")
        Set wsOutput = .Worksheets("OUTPUT")
    End With
    
    ' We set a bunch of range references here, some for use in this routine, and
    ' some so that AutoSize doesn't have to keep referencing the .Range property.
    With wsOutput
        Set rngDirectCost = .Range(RN_DIRECT_COST)
        Set rngValidInput = .Range(RN_OUT_VALID)
    End With
    With ComputedSheet
        Set rngMinVert = .Range(RN_AS_MIN_VERT)
        Set rngAddlNumber = .Range(RN_AS_EXTRA_SEARCH)
        Set rngNLines = .Range(RN_AS_NLINES)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngBedDepth = .Range(RN_COMP_BED_DEPTH)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngWidth = .Range(RN_COMP_LENGTH_WIDTH)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngLength = .Range(RN_COMP_LENGTH_WIDTH)
        Set rngSuccess = .Range(RN_OUT_SUCCESS)
    End With
    
    ' Save the old inputs
    With wsMainInput
        Set MainInputs.rngBedDepth = .Range(RN_IN_BED_DEPTH)
        Set MainInputs.rngWidth = .Range(RN_IN_WIDTH)
        Set MainInputs.rngLength = .Range(RN_IN_LENGTH)
    End With
    With MainInputs
        OldInputs.dBedDepth = .rngBedDepth.Value
        OldInputs.dWidth = .rngWidth.Value
        OldInputs.dLength = .rngLength.Value
    End With
    
    ' Read the search parameters.
    lExtraSearch = rngAddlNumber.Value
    If lExtraSearch < 1 Then lExtraSearch = MIN_EXTRA_SEARCH
    
    ' Now search vertical vessel designs for a minimum cost.
    ' Once we reach a minimum, go at least as far as specified in Extra Search
    ' to verify it.


    ' The target load then lets us determine the minimum number of lines for a
    ' valid vertical design.
    lMinVertNLines = Ceiling(rngMinVert.Value)
    lNLines = lMinVertNLines
    dMinCostVert = HUGE_COST
    lNSinceMin = 0
    lNInvalid = 0
    Do While lNSinceMin < lExtraSearch And lNInvalid < MAX_INVALID
        ' Store the number of lines.
        rngNLines.Value = lNLines
        ' Run autosize.
        AutosizeSucceeded = autosize(ComputedInputs, MainInputs, rngValidInput)
        ' Check whether the autosize procedure succeeded.
        If AutosizeSucceeded Then
            Call CompSelect
            vntDirectCost = rngDirectCost.Value
            If IsNumeric(vntDirectCost) Then
                dDirectCost = CDbl(vntDirectCost)
                If dDirectCost < dMinCostVert Then
                    dMinCostVert = dDirectCost
                    lNLines_MinCostVert = lNLines
                    lNSinceMin = 0
                Else
                    lNSinceMin = lNSinceMin + 1
                End If
            Else
                ' direct cost is non-numeric (probably Excel error value)
                lNInvalid = lNInvalid + 1
            End If
        Else
            ' autosize failed for this number of lines
            lNInvalid = lNInvalid + 1
        End If
        ' And increment the number of lines.
        lNLines = lNLines + 1
    Loop
  
  If dMinCostVert < HUGE_COST Then
        rngNLines.Value = lNLines_MinCostVert
        Call autosize(ComputedInputs, MainInputs, rngValidInput)
        Call CompSelect
        rngSuccess.Value = True
  Else
    ' there was no valid design
        If Interactive Then Call AutoSizeErr("Couldn't find a valid design.")
        With MainInputs
               .rngBedDepth.Value = OldInputs.dBedDepth
               .rngWidth.Value = OldInputs.dWidth
               .rngLength.Value = OldInputs.dLength
        End With
           rngSuccess.Value = False
        End If
    
        
End Sub




' A private ceiling function--we use this to convert the minimum number of lines
' calculations, which are floating-point, to integer minima.
' Ceiling(floatValue) = smallest integer greater than or equal to floatValue
' Therefore:
' Ceiling(1.) = 1
' Ceiling(-3.) = -3
' Ceiling(1.1) = 2
' Ceiling(-3.1) = -3
' Ceiling(0.) = 0
Private Function Ceiling(FloatValue As Double) As Long
    Dim lCeil As Long
    
    lCeil = Int(FloatValue)
    ' If FloatValue was an exact integer, we now have its ceiling, so leave
    ' lCeil alone.  Otherwise, increment lCeil by one to find the next largest
    ' integer.
    If CDbl(lCeil) < FloatValue Then
        lCeil = lCeil + 1
    End If
    Ceiling = lCeil
End Function

' Sub AutoSize
' written 10/2003 by Jon Fetter
'
Private Function autosize(ComputedInputRanges As ComputedInputRangesType, _
                          MainInputRanges As MainInputRangesType, _
                          ValidInputTestRange As Range, _
                          Optional Interactive = False) As Boolean
    Dim NewInputs As InputValuesType
    Dim vntBedDepth As Variant
    Dim ErrorInBedDepth As Boolean
    
    ' Get the new inputs
    With ComputedInputRanges
        vntBedDepth = .rngBedDepth.Value
        If IsError(vntBedDepth) Then
            ErrorInBedDepth = True
        Else
            ErrorInBedDepth = False
            NewInputs.dBedDepth = CDbl(vntBedDepth)
            NewInputs.dWidth = .rngWidth.Value
            NewInputs.dLength = .rngLength.Value

        End If
    End With
    
    ' Check the new inputs
    If ErrorInBedDepth Or NewInputs.dBedDepth < 0# Then
        If Interactive Then Call AutoSizeErr("Could not find a valid bed depth.")
        autosize = False
        Exit Function
    End If
    If NewInputs.dWidth < 0# Then
        If Interactive Then Call AutoSizeErr("Could not find a valid basin width.")
        autosize = False
        Exit Function
    End If
    If NewInputs.dLength < 0# Then
        If Interactive Then Call AutoSizeErr("Could not find a valid length.")
        autosize = False
        Exit Function
    End If
    
    ' Put the new inputs into the input sheet
    With MainInputRanges
        .rngBedDepth.Value = NewInputs.dBedDepth
        .rngWidth.Value = NewInputs.dWidth
        .rngLength.Value = NewInputs.dLength
    End With
    
    ' Check to see that the inputs are valid; if they aren't, restore the old ones
    If Not CheckValidInput(ValidInputTestRange) Then
        autosize = False
        Exit Function
    End If
    
    ' Finally, return True and exit.
    autosize = True
End Function

Private Function CheckValidInput(Valid As Range) As Boolean
    ' this just looks at the true/false entries in the range
    Dim rngCell As Range
    Dim vntCellVal As Variant
    Dim CellVal As Boolean
    
    ' go through each cell, making sure it doesn't contain an error and
    ' then making sure its value is True
    For Each rngCell In Valid.Cells
        vntCellVal = rngCell.Value
        If IsError(vntCellVal) Then
            CheckValidInput = False
            Exit Function
        End If
        CellVal = vntCellVal
        If CellVal = False Then
            CheckValidInput = False
            Exit Function
        End If
    Next rngCell
    
    ' if we got here, everything is OK
    CheckValidInput = True
End Function

Private Sub AutoSizeErr(Message As String)
    Call MsgBox("Could not auto-size the vessel." & vbCrLf & Message, _
                vbExclamation + vbOKOnly)
End Sub

Public Sub AutoSizeInteractiveA()
    Call AutoSize_OptA(True)
End Sub

Public Sub AutoSizeNonInteractiveA()
    Call AutoSize_OptA(False)
End Sub

Private Const RN_AS_NLINES As String = "comp_in_nlines"

Private Const RN_IN_BED_DEPTH As String = "bed_depth"
Private Const RN_IN_TANK_GEOM As String = "tank_geom_I"
Private Const RN_IN_DIAM As String = "comm_diam"
Private Const RN_IN_LH As String = "comm_height_length"

Private Const RN_COMP_BED_DEPTH As String = "comp_bed_depth"
Private Const RN_COMP_TANK_GEOM As String = "comp_tank_geom"
Private Const RN_COMP_VERT_HEIGHT As String = "comp_vert_height"
Private Const RN_COMP_VERT_DIAM As String = "comp_vert_diam"

Private Const RN_COMP_HORIZ_DIAM As String = "comp_horiz_diam"

Private Const RN_DIRECT_COST As String = "annualized_cost"
Private Const RN_OUT_SUCCESS As String = "comp_out_success"

Private Const HUGE_COST As Double = 999999999#
Private Const MAX_INVALID As Long = 5
Private Const MIN_EXTRA_SEARCH As Long = 1

    dBedDepth As Double

'
' Sub AutoSize_Opt
' Jon Fetter-Degges, 3/2005
'
Private Sub AutoSize_Opt(Optional Interactive As Boolean = False)
    ' Worksheets
    Dim ComputedSheet As Worksheet
    Dim wsMainInput As Worksheet
    Dim wsOutput As Worksheet
    ' Ranges on the Output sheet
    Dim rngDirectCost As Range
    Dim rngValidInput As Range          ' machine-readable input checks
    ' Ranges on the Autosize sheet
    Dim rngMinVert As Range             ' "min number for vertical vessel" cell on the autosize sheet
    Dim rngMinLong As Range             ' "min number for horizontal vessel" cell on the autosize sheet
    Dim rngAddlNumber As Range          ' "extra search" cell on the autosize sheet
    Dim rngNLines As Range              ' "number of lines" range on autosize sheet
    Dim rngTargetLoadIn As Range
    Dim ComputedInputs As ComputedInputRangesType
    Dim rngSuccess As Range
    ' Ranges and values on the Input sheet
    Dim OldInputs As InputValuesType
    Dim MainInputs As MainInputRangesType
    ' Values from the Autosize sheet
    Dim lExtraSearch As Long
    Dim lMinVertNLines As Long
    Dim lMinLongNLines As Long
    ' Variables used in the search
    Dim lNLines As Long
    Dim lNSinceMin As Long
    Dim lNInvalid As Long
    Dim dMinCostVert As Double
    Dim lNLines_MinCostVert As Long
    Dim dMinCostLong As Double
    Dim lNLines_MinCostLong As Long
    ' Results
    Dim AutosizeSucceeded As Boolean
    Dim vntDirectCost As Variant
    Dim dDirectCost As Double

    With ThisWorkbook
        Set ComputedSheet = .Worksheets("AutoSize")
        Set wsMainInput = .Worksheets("INPUT")
        Set wsOutput = .Worksheets("OUTPUT")
    End With
    
    ' We set a bunch of range references here, some for use in this routine, and
    ' some so that AutoSize doesn't have to keep referencing the .Range property.
    With wsOutput
        Set rngDirectCost = .Range(RN_DIRECT_COST)
        Set rngValidInput = .Range(RN_OUT_VALID)
    End With
    With ComputedSheet
        Set rngMinVert = .Range(RN_AS_MIN_VERT)
        Set rngMinLong = .Range(RN_AS_MIN_LONG)
        Set rngAddlNumber = .Range(RN_AS_EXTRA_SEARCH)
        Set rngNLines = .Range(RN_AS_NLINES)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngBedDepth = .Range(RN_COMP_BED_DEPTH)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngLDiam = .Range(RN_COMP_HORIZ_DIAM)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngLLength = .Range(RN_COMP_HORIZ_LENGTH)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngTankGeom = .Range(RN_COMP_TANK_GEOM)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngVDiam = .Range(RN_COMP_VERT_DIAM)
        Set ComputedInputs.rngVHeight = .Range(RN_COMP_VERT_HEIGHT)
        Set rngSuccess = .Range(RN_OUT_SUCCESS)
    End With
    
    ' Save the old inputs
    With wsMainInput
        Set MainInputs.rngBedDepth = .Range(RN_IN_BED_DEPTH)
        Set MainInputs.rngTankGeom = .Range(RN_IN_TANK_GEOM)
        Set MainInputs.rngDiam = .Range(RN_IN_DIAM)
        Set MainInputs.rngLengthHeight = .Range(RN_IN_LH)
    End With
    With MainInputs
        OldInputs.dBedDepth = .rngBedDepth.Value
        OldInputs.dDiam = .rngDiam.Value
        OldInputs.dLengthHeight = .rngLengthHeight.Value
        OldInputs.sTankGeom = .rngTankGeom.Value
    End With
    
    ' Read the search parameters.
    lExtraSearch = rngAddlNumber.Value
    If lExtraSearch < 1 Then lExtraSearch = MIN_EXTRA_SEARCH
    
    ' Now search vertical vessel designs for a minimum cost.
    ' Once we reach a minimum, go at least as far as specified in Extra Search
    ' to verify it.


    ' The target load then lets us determine the minimum number of lines for a
    ' valid vertical design.
    lMinVertNLines = Ceiling(rngMinVert.Value)
    lNLines = lMinVertNLines
    dMinCostVert = HUGE_COST
    lNSinceMin = 0
    lNInvalid = 0
    Do While lNSinceMin < lExtraSearch And lNInvalid < MAX_INVALID
        ' Store the number of lines.
        rngNLines.Value = lNLines
        ' Run autosize.
        AutosizeSucceeded = autosize(ComputedInputs, MainInputs, rngValidInput)
        ' Check whether the autosize procedure succeeded.
        If AutosizeSucceeded Then
            Call CompSelect
            vntDirectCost = rngDirectCost.Value
            If IsNumeric(vntDirectCost) Then
                dDirectCost = CDbl(vntDirectCost)
                If dDirectCost < dMinCostVert Then
                    dMinCostVert = dDirectCost
                    lNLines_MinCostVert = lNLines
                    lNSinceMin = 0
                Else
                    lNSinceMin = lNSinceMin + 1
                End If
            Else
                ' direct cost is non-numeric (probably Excel error value)
                lNInvalid = lNInvalid + 1
            End If
        Else
            ' autosize failed for this number of lines
            lNInvalid = lNInvalid + 1
        End If
        ' And increment the number of lines.
        lNLines = lNLines + 1
    Loop
    
    ' Now do the same for horizontal designs.
    lMinLongNLines = Ceiling(rngMinLong.Value)
    lNLines = lMinLongNLines
    dMinCostLong = HUGE_COST
    lNSinceMin = 0
    lNInvalid = 0
    Do While lNSinceMin < lExtraSearch _
             And lNInvalid < MAX_INVALID _
             And lNLines < lMinVertNLines
        ' Store the number of lines.
        rngNLines.Value = lNLines
        ' Run autosize.
        AutosizeSucceeded = autosize(ComputedInputs, MainInputs, rngValidInput)
        ' Check whether the autosize procedure succeeded.
        If AutosizeSucceeded Then
            Call CompSelect
            dDirectCost = rngDirectCost.Value
            If dDirectCost < dMinCostLong Then
                dMinCostLong = dDirectCost
                lNLines_MinCostLong = lNLines
                lNSinceMin = 0
            Else
                lNSinceMin = lNSinceMin + 1
            End If
        Else
            lNInvalid = lNInvalid + 1
        End If
        ' And increment the number of lines.
        lNLines = lNLines + 1
    Loop
    
    ' Now determine which design to use, and run it.
    If dMinCostVert < dMinCostLong Then
        ' vertical design was optimal
        rngNLines.Value = lNLines_MinCostVert
        Call autosize(ComputedInputs, MainInputs, rngValidInput)
        Call CompSelect
        rngSuccess.Value = True
    Else
        ' either longitudinal vessels are preferred, or they're both HUGE_COST,
        ' i.e., there was no valid design
        If dMinCostLong < HUGE_COST Then
            ' longitudinal vessels are preferred
            rngNLines.Value = lNLines_MinCostLong
            Call autosize(ComputedInputs, MainInputs, rngValidInput)
            Call CompSelect
            rngSuccess.Value = True
        Else
            ' there was no valid design
            If Interactive Then Call AutoSizeErr("Couldn't find a valid design.")
            With MainInputs
                .rngBedDepth.Value = OldInputs.dBedDepth
                .rngDiam.Value = OldInputs.dDiam
                .rngLengthHeight.Value = OldInputs.dLengthHeight
                .rngTankGeom.Value = OldInputs.sTankGeom
            End With
            rngSuccess.Value = False
        End If
    End If
End Sub

' A private ceiling function--we use this to convert the minimum number of lines
' calculations, which are floating-point, to integer minima.
' Ceiling(floatValue) = smallest integer greater than or equal to floatValue
' Therefore:
' Ceiling(1.) = 1
' Ceiling(-3.) = -3
' Ceiling(1.1) = 2
' Ceiling(-3.1) = -3
' Ceiling(0.) = 0
Private Function Ceiling(FloatValue As Double) As Long
    Dim lCeil As Long
    
    lCeil = Int(FloatValue)
    ' If FloatValue was an exact integer, we now have its ceiling, so leave
    ' lCeil alone.  Otherwise, increment lCeil by one to find the next largest
    ' integer.
    If CDbl(lCeil) < FloatValue Then
        lCeil = lCeil + 1
    End If
    Ceiling = lCeil
End Function

' Sub AutoSize
' written 10/2003 by Jon Fetter
'
Private Function autosize(ComputedInputRanges As ComputedInputRangesType, _
                          MainInputRanges As MainInputRangesType, _
                          ValidInputTestRange As Range, _
                          Optional Interactive = False) As Boolean
    Dim NewInputs As InputValuesType
    Dim vntBedDepth As Variant
    Dim ErrorInBedDepth As Boolean
    
    ' Get the new inputs
    With ComputedInputRanges
        vntBedDepth = .rngBedDepth.Value
        If IsError(vntBedDepth) Then
            ErrorInBedDepth = True
        Else
            ErrorInBedDepth = False
            NewInputs.dBedDepth = CDbl(vntBedDepth)
            NewInputs.sTankGeom = .rngTankGeom.Value
            Select Case NewInputs.sTankGeom
            Case "upright"
                NewInputs.dDiam = .rngVDiam.Value
                NewInputs.dLengthHeight = .rngVHeight.Value
            Case "horizontal"
                NewInputs.dDiam = .rngLDiam.Value
                NewInputs.dLengthHeight = .rngLLength.Value
            Case Else
                NewInputs.dDiam = -1#
                NewInputs.dLengthHeight = -1#
            End Select
        End If
    End With
    
    ' Check the new inputs
    If ErrorInBedDepth Or NewInputs.dBedDepth < 0# Then
        If Interactive Then Call AutoSizeErr("Could not find a valid bed depth.")
        autosize = False
        Exit Function
    End If
    If NewInputs.dDiam < 0# Then
        If Interactive Then Call AutoSizeErr("Could not find a valid tank diameter.")
        autosize = False
        Exit Function
    End If
    If NewInputs.dLengthHeight < 0# Then
        If Interactive Then Call AutoSizeErr("Could not find a valid tank length or height.")
        autosize = False
        Exit Function
    End If
    
    ' Put the new inputs into the input sheet
    With MainInputRanges
        .rngBedDepth.Value = NewInputs.dBedDepth
        .rngTankGeom.Value = NewInputs.sTankGeom
        .rngDiam.Value = NewInputs.dDiam
        .rngLengthHeight.Value = NewInputs.dLengthHeight
    End With
    
    ' Check to see that the inputs are valid; if they aren't, restore the old ones
    If Not CheckValidInput(ValidInputTestRange) Then
        autosize = False
        Exit Function
    End If
    
    ' Finally, return True and exit.
    autosize = True
End Function

Private Function CheckValidInput(Valid As Range) As Boolean
    ' this just looks at the true/false entries in the range
    Dim rngCell As Range
    Dim vntCellVal As Variant
    Dim CellVal As Boolean
    
    ' go through each cell, making sure it doesn't contain an error and
    ' then making sure its value is True
    For Each rngCell In Valid.Cells
        vntCellVal = rngCell.Value
        If IsError(vntCellVal) Then
            CheckValidInput = False
            Exit Function
        End If
        CellVal = vntCellVal
        If CellVal = False Then
            CheckValidInput = False
            Exit Function
        End If
    Next rngCell
    
    ' if we got here, everything is OK
    CheckValidInput = True
End Function

Private Sub AutoSizeErr(Message As String)
    Call MsgBox("Could not auto-size the vessel." & vbCrLf & Message, _
                vbExclamation + vbOKOnly)
End Sub

    End If

' code to plug in standard input values from the Standard Inputs sheet

' worksheet names (suffixed with WSN)
Private Const IN_WSN As String = "INPUT"

Private Const STDIN_RN As String = "std_design_params"

Private Sub StdInputs(DesignNum As Long)
    Dim wsStdIn As Worksheet
    Dim wsInput As Worksheet
    Dim rngStdIn As Range
    Dim lNRows As Long, lNCols As Long
    Dim lRowIndx As Long, lColIndx As Long
    Dim sInputRN As String
    Dim vntInputVal As Variant
    Dim nmContamRngNm As Name    '*contaminant choice
    Dim lContamNum As Long       '*contaminant choice
    Dim nmDesignRngNm As Name    '*design choice
    Dim DesignType As Long       '*design choice
    Dim ClearDesign As Boolean
    Dim InvalidDesign As Boolean
    
    ' get references to worksheets and ranges
    With ThisWorkbook
        Set wsStdIn = .Worksheets(STDIN_WSN)
        Set wsInput = .Worksheets(IN_WSN)
        Set nmContamRngNm = .Names(CONTAM_RN) '*contaminant choice
        Set nmDesignRngNm = .Names(DESIGN_RN) '*design choice
    End With
    Set rngStdIn = wsStdIn.Range(STDIN_RN)
    
    ' get contaminant selection
    lContamNum = nmContamRngNm.RefersToRange  '*contaminant choice
    ' get design selection (gravity or pressure)
    DesignType = nmDesignRngNm.RefersToRange  '*design choice
    
    With rngStdIn
        ' count rows and columns
        lNRows = .Rows.Count
        lNCols = .Columns.Count
        
        If DesignNum = CLEAR_DESIGN_NUM Then
            ClearDesign = True
            InvalidDesign = False
        Else
            ClearDesign = False
            ' find the appropriate row of the standard input range: loop through
            '  cells of the first column, jump out of the loop when we find a cell
            '  which matches the DesignNum passed in
            ' don't check row 1 because it just contains range names
            For lRowIndx = 2 To lNRows
                If Val(.Cells(lRowIndx, 1)) = lContamNum _
                    And Val(.Cells(lRowIndx, 2)) = DesignType _
                    And Val(.Cells(lRowIndx, 3)) = DesignNum Then Exit For '*media choice: was col 1 -> design num only
            Next lRowIndx
        
            ' did we find a matching row?
            If lRowIndx > lNRows Then
                ' we didn't find a matching row--just exit silently
                ' but we're inside a With, so set a flag to turn off further
                ' behavior rather than jumping out
                InvalidDesign = True
            Else
                InvalidDesign = False
            End If
        End If
        
        ' unless InvalidDesign is true, lRowIndx contains the row with the
        ' standard inputs. we now look at each column (excluding the first
        ' one, because it just contains design numbers).  for each row, the
        ' first row contains the range name (on the input sheet) that we are
        ' to fill.  the lRowIndx row contains the value to be plugged in.  if
        ' the first row is blank, we skip this column.  likewise, if the value
        ' is -- (two dashes), we skip the column.  if the value is blank, we
        ' just plug in a blank value on the input sheet.
        ' we wrap the whole thing in an On Error Resume Next so that incorrect
        ' range names will not cause crashes--instead we will silently fail to
        ' fill in those values.
        If Not InvalidDesign Then
            On Error Resume Next
                For lColIndx = 3 To lNCols  '*r choice: 2->3
                    sInputRN = .Cells(1, lColIndx)
                    If ClearDesign Then
                        Call wsInput.Range(sInputRN).ClearContents
                    Else
                        vntInputVal = .Cells(lRowIndx, lColIndx)
                        If Not (Len(sInputRN) = 0 Or vntInputVal = "--") Then
                            wsInput.Range(sInputRN) = vntInputVal
                        End If
                    End If
                Next lColIndx
            On Error GoTo 0
        End If
        
        ' that's it!
    End With
End Sub

'***StoreInputs added MRG 3-20-06
'* StoreInputs records the inputs used for CompSelect.  It reads the input
'  range names from the standard inputs table, retrieves the current value of
'  each range, and stores the value in a range on the Standard Inputs sheet.
Public Sub StoreInputs()
    Dim wsStdIn As Worksheet
    Dim rngStdInRNs As Range
    Dim vntRangeNames As Variant
    Dim lNCols As Long
    Dim lCol As Long
    Dim sThisInputRN As String
    Dim rngThisInput As Range
    Dim vntCurrentInputs() As Variant
    Dim rngCurrentInputs As Range
    
    Set wsStdIn = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets(STDIN_WSN)
    With wsStdIn
        Set rngStdInRNs = .Range(STDIN_RN).Rows(1)
        Set rngCurrentInputs = .Range(INP_STORE_RN)
    End With
    With rngStdInRNs
        lNCols = .Columns.Count
        vntRangeNames = .Value
    End With
    ReDim vntCurrentInputs(1 To 1, 1 To lNCols) As Variant
    
    On Error Resume Next
    With ThisWorkbook.Names
        For lCol = 1 To lNCols
            sThisInputRN = vntRangeNames(1, lCol)
            ' use only columns with a range name in the header
            If Len(sThisInputRN) > 0 Then
                Set rngThisInput = .Item(sThisInputRN).RefersToRange
                vntCurrentInputs(1, lCol) = rngThisInput.Value
                If Err.Number > 0 Then
                    vntCurrentInputs(1, lCol) = "RANGE ERROR"
                    Call Err.Clear
                ElseIf IsError(vntCurrentInputs(1, lCol)) Then
                    vntCurrentInputs(1, lCol) = "VALUE ERROR"
                End If
            End If
        Next lCol
    End With
    
    rngCurrentInputs.Value = vntCurrentInputs
End Sub

    Call RestoreInputs1
    Call RestoreInputs2
    Call RestoreInputs3
    Call RestoreInputs4
    Call RestoreInputs5
    Call RestoreInputs6
    Call RestoreInputs6
    Call RestoreInputs7
    
    End Sub

Attribute VB_Name = "Bldg_Pmt_Cost_Macro"
Option Explicit
Public Const ElecValuationPercent As Double = 0.09  'Based on medium quality building with heating and cooling (ranges from 8.8%-9.3% over 500-50,000 sf)
Public Const MechValuationPercent As Double = 0.327 'Based on medium quality building with heating and cooling
Public Const HeatValuationPercent As Double = 0.095 'Based on medium quality building with heating and cooling
Public Const CoolingValuationPercent As Double = 0.232 'Based on medium quality building with heating and cooling

Function RoundUp0(X As Double) As Double
'slower, but much less to write out - makes things clearer...
RoundUp0 = Application.WorksheetFunction.RoundUp(X, 0)
End Function

Function BPCost_UBC97(Valuation As Double) As Double

Select Case Valuation
    Case 0
        BPCost_UBC97 = 0
    Case 1 To 500
        BPCost_UBC97 = 23.5
    Case 501 To 2000
        BPCost_UBC97 = 23.5 + RoundUp0((Valuation - 500) / 100) * 3.05
    Case 2001 To 25000
        BPCost_UBC97 = 69.25 + RoundUp0((Valuation - 2000) / 1000) * 14
    Case 25001 To 50000
        BPCost_UBC97 = 391.75 + RoundUp0((Valuation - 25000) / 1000) * 10.1
    Case 50001 To 100000
        BPCost_UBC97 = 643.75 + RoundUp0((Valuation - 50000) / 1000) * 7
    Case 100001 To 500000
        BPCost_UBC97 = 993.75 + RoundUp0((Valuation - 100000) / 1000) * 5.6
    Case 500001 To 1000000
        BPCost_UBC97 = 3233.75 + RoundUp0((Valuation - 500000) / 1000) * 4.75
    Case Is > 1000000
        BPCost_UBC97 = 5608.75 + RoundUp0((Valuation - 1000000) / 1000) * 3.65
    End Select
End Function

Attribute VB_Name = "CheckAutosize"
' 3/31/22  SS  CheckOptimalFit
Public Sub CheckOptimalFit(Optional ByVal rngA As String = "inp_autosize", _
                            Optional ByVal rngB As String = "inp_used_vessels", _
                            Optional ByVal autosize As String = "AutosizeInteractive")
    Dim wsStdInputs As Worksheet
    Dim optimalRange As Range
    Dim currentRange As Range
    Dim idx As Integer
    Dim answer As Integer
    
    Set wsStdInputs = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Standard Inputs")
    With wsStdInputs
        Set optimalRange = .Range(rngA)
        Set currentRange = .Range(rngB)
    End With
    
    idx = optimalRange.Count
    While idx > 0
        
        If Not optimalRange.Cells.Item(idx) = currentRange.Cells.Item(idx) Then
            If (currentRange.Cells.Item(idx).Value = "") Then
                Exit Sub ' Exit if blank, since it means another autosize is enabled
            End If
            answer = MsgBox("Contactor size may not be optimal." & vbNewLine & _
                            "Do you want to run autosize?" & vbNewLine & _
                            "Click No to run with current values.", vbYesNo + vbQuestion)
            If answer = vbYes Then
                Run (autosize)
                Exit Sub
            Else
                Exit Sub
            End If
        End If
        idx = idx - 1
    Wend
End Sub
